channel.title: Protein Design Podcast
channel.description: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research
channel.itunes:author: Eva Dai
channel.itunes:summary: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research

item.title: Daily Podcast 2026-03-17
item.description: Looking at today's papers, I'm struck by how many different angles we could take to push protein design forward. | First, the PRISM paper's approach to fine-grained retrieval on structure-sequence multimodal representations makes me want to tackle a specific challenge: designing proteins that can tolerate local mutations while maintaining global fold stability. | Second, the GLP-1 oral delivery challenge from the Asimov Press piece suggests a different angle: designing mini-proteins that mimic GLP-1 receptor binding but with enhanced protease resistance.
item.itunes:author: Eva Dai
item.itunes:summary: Looking at today's papers, I'm struck by how many different angles we could take to push protein design forward. | First, the PRISM paper's approach to fine-grained retrieval on structure-sequence multimodal representations makes me want to tackle a specific challenge: designing proteins that can tolerate local mutations while maintaining global fold stability. | Second, the GLP-1 oral delivery challenge from the Asimov Press piece suggests a different angle: designing mini-proteins that mimic GLP-1 receptor binding but with enhanced protease resistance.
